geisa-application-manifest.$comment: Example deployment manifest derived from the vendor application manifest. Shows operator-approved effective settings for deployment on a specific class of platform.

manifest.$comment: Application identity and compatibility are preserved from the vendor application manifest unless explicitly overridden below.
manifest.author: GEISA Developer
manifest.name: Awesome App
manifest.description: This app makes your device awesome

artifacts.$comment: Deployment continues to use the same application artifact as the vendor manifest in this example.

api-access.$comment: API access is accepted unchanged from the vendor application manifest in this example.

compatibility.$comment: Compatibility requirements are preserved from the vendor application manifest.

default-configuration.$comment: Operator override of vendor defaults. Volume is reduced and one feature is disabled for this deployment.

default-launch-strategy.$comment: Operator-adjusted restart behavior. Restart count is reduced, restart window is increased, and notify timeout is increased. Final enforcement remains subject to platform policy.

communication.$comment: Operator-restricted communication limits relative to the vendor manifest.

messaging.$comment: Deployment reduces permitted daily message-bus usage.

local.$comment: Deployment reduces allowed local network daily volume while preserving the same outbound port access.

resources.$comment: Operator-adjusted resource allocation. CPU and persistent storage are reduced relative to the vendor manifest while other minimum resources remain unchanged.
